subject: Geography
title: Borders After Wars
description: A quick win: understand Borders After Wars.

hook.text: After World War I, the Ottoman Empire was carved into new nations by European diplomats.

buildup.text: Sykes and Picot drew lines across the Middle East with little regard for local communities.

discovery.text: Iraq, Syria, Jordan, and Lebanon were all created from those lines.

twist.text: Kurdish people, 40 million strong, were split across four countries with no state of their own.

climax.text: Wars redraw borders, but the new lines often plant the seeds of the next conflict.

punchline.text: Win the war, draw the map, and wait for the next war.

quiz.question: What happened to the Kurdish people after WWI border drawing?
options: They were split across four countries without their own state
